item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this idea, that would be a really interesting interplay. Overall I completely agree, except I think the fact that there isnt really much here isnt a bad thing, not every anomaly has to be crazy and have layers of mystery. That's just my opinion though, I can understand why you didn't vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i feel like it needs a bit more, but otherwise pretty nice.

i always liked the idea of mundane things being powerful reality anchors for no explainable reason, just because of the immense technology that is needed to make an SRA work and it makes a good mystery as to what made it so powerful.

like 1000+ humes is a crazy amount and it makes you wonder why. +1

unrelated but i think it would be cool if someone made a sort of 'chart' of certain anomalies and they're hume level. for example 3001 and 3480 would be at the very bottom and just above that 682 and at the very end would be 343 and possibly this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me, I like how this comes in and says what it needs to say and then leaves. I do agree with what everyone else is saying, this could use just a little more to push it into greatness, but right now it is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I would like this better if it weren't at a Foundation site. Its presence there detracts from the overall article because we don't really wonder why it was made there. If it's some kind of mirror into a parallel reality, maybe it's at a company and some of the employees are swapping places with their counterparts. That could be a story. I have to agree that although it's got the makings of a strong article it feels lesser than the sum of its parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels a bit pot-boiler to me, but in a good way; it's a fun, silly little idea that doesn't need to be thoroughly explored or otherwise expanded on. The addition of personnel appearing and disappearing at the end is what got me to upvote (albeit just barely).

I feel like there's a few more twists and turns that could have been thrown in here, but maybe not?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The anomaly is interesting, but it just isn't enough to support the entire article.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is gonna be a no-vote for me, there's just not enough substance. However it is well written it just doesn't have the narrative oomph for me to up vote.

However while reading this it came upon me what if this is used as a thaumiel? Think about it, un-damageable, endless amounts of food and water, etc. What if a world ending scenario occurred and a fucking break room was the last stronghold of humanity? This would be amazing addenda for what this scp is lacking. And that's just 1 thought, it just needs more narrative.

item.title: I have had a latte every morning for the past five days.
